{
  "document_metadata": {
    "board": "",
    "subject": "Science",
    "class": "7",
    "grade": "Grade 7",
    "stream": "",
    "academic_year": "",
    "total_pages": ""
  },
  "classes": [
    {
      "class_name": "Class 7",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": ""
    }
  ],
  "units": [
    {
      "class_name": "Class 7",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "",
      "unit_id": "U1",
      "unit_name": "Exploring Mixtures and their Separation",
      "marks": null,
      "topics": [
        "Homogeneous and heterogeneous mixtures; Solutions, suspensions and colloids and their properties",
        "Various ways to express concentration of solutions (mass by mass percentage of a solution, mass by volume percentage of a solution, volume by volume percentage of a solution)",
        "Separation techniques based on the physical properties of components"
      ],
      "subtopics": [
        "crystallisation",
        "distillation",
        "paper chromatography",
        "sublimation",
        "centrifugation",
        "coagulation"
      ],
      "key_concepts": [
        "Differentiate between homogeneous and heterogeneous mixtures on the basis of their properties",
        "Demonstrate separation techniques, such as crystallisation, distillation, paper chromatography, sublimation, centrifugation and coagulation",
        "Classify mixtures as solutions, suspensions, or colloids based on their properties",
        "Explain the scientific principles behind different separation techniques",
        "Apply the knowledge of homogeneous and heterogeneous mixtures in daily life",
        "Define and calculate the concentration of solutions using mass by mass percentage, mass by volume percentage, volume by volume percentage",
        "Analyse graphs of solubility and explain how the solubility of substances changes with temperature",
        "Use scientific conventions and standard units to express concentrations",
        "Handle common laboratory chemicals and apparatus safely",
        "Relate separation techniques with practices observed in the local environment"
      ]
    },
    {
      "class_name": "Class 7",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "",
      "unit_id": "U2",
      "unit_name": "Structure of an Atom",
      "marks": null,
      "topics": [
        "Atoms are the basic units of elements; Atoms consist of subatomic particles"
      ],
      "subtopics": [
        "Thomson's Model",
        "Rutherford's Model",
        "Bohr's Model",
        "Distributions of electrons in elements (up to 18 elements)",
        "Symbols",
        "Valency as the number of electrons in a shell, and combining capacity",
        "Atomic number",
        "Mass number",
        "Isotopes",
        "Isobars"
      ],
      "key_concepts": [
        "Differentiate between subatomic particles (electrons, protons, and neutrons) based on their charge, and position in the atom",
        "Illustrate how electrons are distributed in different energy levels, such as K, L, M, N ... or by numbers n = 1, 2, 3, 4 ...",
        "Explain valence electrons, valency, atomic number, atomic mass, isotopes, and isobars",
        "Calculate the number of electrons, protons, and neutrons of an element using its atomic and mass numbers",
        "Interpret data, such as atomic mass, maximum number of electrons in a shell, and valency to classify elements accurately",
        "Use scientific conventions as per international standards, such as notations for electron, proton, neutron, unified atomic mass unit (u), and distribution of electrons in various shells"
      ]
    },
    {
      "class_name": "Class 7",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "",
      "unit_id": "U3",
      "unit_name": "Atoms and Molecules",
      "marks": null,
      "topics": [
        "Law of conservation of mass; Law of constant proportion"
      ],
      "subtopics": [
        "Dalton's Atomic theory",
        "Molecules of elements, Molecules of covalent compounds and their properties",
        "Ions, Ionic compounds and their properties",
        "Writing chemical formulae",
        "Molecular mass",
        "Formula unit mass"
      ],
      "key_concepts": [
        "Differentiate between chemical species based on their properties or characteristics, such as atoms and molecules, elements and compounds, ionic and covalent compounds, cations and anions, formula unit mass and molecular mass",
        "Plan and demonstrate activities to observe and verify the law of conservation of mass",
        "Explain Dalton's atomic theory, the law of conservation of mass, the law of constant proportions, and formation of ionic and covalent compounds",
        "Calculate the charge on an ion, valency from the atomic number, the molecular and formula unit mass",
        "Use scientific conventions, symbols, and valency to write the chemical formulae of simple compounds"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "chapters": []
}
